content:
Hi All.

We are getting a ton of build errors from the intel robot failing to build upstream due to missing fields:

  • https://lore.kernel.org/r/202512021007.njLxXEZ1-lkp@intel.com
  • https://lore.kernel.org/r/202512020943.whFrDsXx-lkp@intel.com
  • https://lore.kernel.org/r/202512020454.Tf36WHw5-lkp@intel.com
  • https://lore.kernel.org/r/202511220744.cA12gIBc-lkp@intel.com
  • and more

They seem to hit all subsystems. @Daniel Gomez and I have been trying to reproduce the ones we got for modules, but no success.

Does anyone have a clue as to what is going on?
@team
Looks like there're some issues with bindgen? I couldn't think on top of my head on what might went wrong though, as configfs_system just have a single definition and isn't ifdef'd
We drew the same conclusion for kernel_param_ops for the modules report.
Do you know how to check what bindgen they are using? I was grepping the lkp repo, but there is no mention of bindgen.
I think version numbers are in the config file

CONFIG_RUSTC_VERSION_TEXT="rustc 1.88.0 (6b00bc388 2025-06-23)"
CONFIG_BINDGEN_VERSION_TEXT="bindgen 0.72.0"
Ok, I am running 71. I'll check 72.
One possibility is using old bindgen with new LLVM
Oh 0.72 is the latest
Yea, but I think rustc 1.88.0 uses clang 20 but bindgen 0.72 uses clang 21.
Or is that a function of whoever builds the binaries?
It is (rustc supports several LLVM major versions and bindgen supports several libclangs and can be linked differently depending on how you configure it).
The report from build bot seems to indicate it's using clang 22 w/ bindgen 0.72
We have to assume they build their bindgen with the same clang version as they use for rustc
(The build process warns if the libclang vs. Clang versions do not match)
I think bindgen will try to find clang dynamic library

Yeah. They also allow static linking it, and the whole setup is a bit messy -- there are a few env vars involved. In the docs I link to:

https://github.com/KyleMayes/clang-sys#linking
https://github.com/KyleMayes/clang-sys#environment-variables
I managed to build with rustc 1.88.0, bindgen 0.72.1 and libclang 20 for both. No issues with that.
I think I have to build rustc from source if I want to use libclang22.
I don't think it'll be rustc at fault. Perhaps just try getting bindgen to use libclang22?
llvm22 is not in nixpkgs yet, so it's not so easy
Yeah rustc doesn't even run until after bindgen, so bad bindgen output doesn't require matching rustc version
Actually LLVM 22 isn't released yet
So it's an early warning kind of build?
lkp downloads llvm-22
Are any of you set up to build llvm from source ?

Daniel Gomez said:

lkp downloads llvm-22

Can you see where they get it from?
I think you just need cmake & ninja & python3 to build LLVM

@Andreas Hindborg
i've got it in ~/0day following the reproducer steps:
https://download.01.org/0day-ci/archive/20251121/202511210858.uwVivgvn-lkp@intel.com/reproduce

Daniel Gomez said:

Andreas Hindborg
i've got it in ~/0day following the reproducer steps:
https://download.01.org/0day-ci/archive/20251121/202511210858.uwVivgvn-lkp@intel.com/reproduce

I see. Are you able to use those details to reproduce the failure?
not yet. I haven't tried to build/cross arm.

i mean
these are from the um failure
and i couldn't reproduce it
And @David Gow couldn't either
Did you download their toolchain or use something else?
first i use older version, then i use their toolchain
Where can you download their binaries?
IIRC the COMPILER_INSTALL_PATH=$HOME/0day COMPILER=clang-22 ~/lkp-tests/kbuild/make.cross W=1 O=build_dir ARCH=um prepare prepare step download it
OK cool.
I wonder if that downloads bindgen as well, or if you ended up using your system bindgen

confirmed:

COMPILER_INSTALL_PATH=$HOME/0day COMPILER=clang-22 ~/lkp-tests/kbuild/make.cross W=1 O=build_dir ARCH=um prepare
Compiler will be installed in /home/dagomez/0day
PATH=/home/dagomez/0day/llvm-22.0.0-e19fa930ca838715028c00c234874d1db4f93154-20250918-184558-x86_64/bin:/home/dagomez/.local/bin:/home/dagomez/go/bin:/home/dagomez/.cargo/bin:/home/dagomez/.local/bin:/home/dagomez/.local/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/local/games:/usr/games
make --keep-going LLVM=1 CROSS_COMPILE=um-linux- --jobs=32 KCFLAGS= -Wno-error=return-type -Wreturn-type -funsigned-char -Wundef W=1 O=build_dir ARCH=um prepare
make[1]: Entering directory...
Can you check in the config where it gets bindgen from?
checking
aha! i don't have bindgen in my build box
OK, so even with their bindgen, you cannot reproduce this issue.
It is sort of hard mode debugging
no bindgen at all

Daniel Gomez said:

no bindgen at all

You cannot build the rust parts without bindgen

grep -i "bindgen\|rustc" /tmp/kernel/modules/.config
CONFIG_RUSTC_VERSION=109101
CONFIG_RUSTC_LLVM_VERSION=210102
CONFIG_RUSTC_HAS_COERCE_POINTEE=y
CONFIG_RUSTC_HAS_SPAN_FILE=y
CONFIG_RUSTC_HAS_UNNECESSARY_TRANSMUTES=y
CONFIG_RUSTC_HAS_FILE_WITH_NUL=y
CONFIG_RUSTC_HAS_FILE_AS_C_STR=y
Ah, rust parts get disabled automatically if you don't have the tools.
I've been hit by that a lot. can you run make rustavailable target with all their settings?
If it says no, it will silently disable rust from your config, and you would not see any rust related errors.

yes, now i see this

make: *** [Makefile:248: __sub-make] Error 2
but this is not the machine i used for modules/rust testing. But it is the machine i use for um testing
my bad
No worries, it is a ridiculous feature.
The silent disable thing.
Can you get bindgen 0.72.0 from github and try again?
I think you can cargo install it
rust docs says (for debian) use package manager so I went first with that. Let me build from source after...

Actually Alice posted this 10 days ago in another topic
I couldn’t reproduce the errors after applying the fixes. But I still don’t understand how 0day managed to avoid them? Or if the reproducer is actually not what they run?
I sent this: https://lore.kernel.org/all/3447801b-95f7-4368-a433-e79669b5d22e@kernel.org/

Yeah, I couldn't reproduce this. The 'Don't rename vmap' issue above is a UML-specific issue with very similar symptoms, but definitely isn't the same thing.

I do wonder if it's caused by an issue in bindgen 0.72.0: https://github.com/rust-lang/rust-bindgen/issues/3275 — though again, I've been totally unable to repoduce it even with that version.
I think I encountered bindgen related problem too here https://lore.kernel.org/all/20251202132016.653e1fa0@nimda.home/

I tried reproducing the configfs arm32 missing fields problem with bindgen built against upstream clang 22:

bindgen --version --verbose
bindgen 0.72.1
Clang: clang version 22.0.0git

But I still do not get the error.

There was a few reports of the same errors still being present this night:

  • https://lore.kernel.org/r/202512031349.FAjXZLVU-lkp@intel.com
  • https://lore.kernel.org/r/202512031542.URVb8ITS-lkp@intel.com
For arm32

I couldn't reproduce that one either, but I haven't tried the combination of clang 22 and bindgen 0.72.0 (which seems to have an issue very much like this — https://github.com/rust-lang/rust-bindgen/issues/3264 — which was fixed in 0.72.1).
